TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: aitsu a maretseketsekeɫ inika tadjalan a mamazangiɫan katua qaqetitan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a marəʦəkəʦəkəʎ inika taɉaɭan a mamazaŋiʎan katua qaqətitan.
S.FORM: aicu a marecekecekelj inika tadjalan a mamazangiljan katua qaqetitan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a marəʦəkəʦəkəʎ inika taɉaɭan a mamazaŋiʎan katua qaqətitan.
S.TRANSL: Weddings of chieftains and commoners are not the same.

S.FORM: nu mamazangiɫan marepaukuz aya.
S.PHON: nu mamazaŋiʎan marəpaukuz aja.
S.FORM: nu mamazangiljan marepaukuz aya.
S.PHON: nu mamazaŋiʎan marəpaukuz aja.
S.TRANSL: With chieftains they are known as marepaukuz;

S.FORM: nu qaqetitan marepalikuz aya.
S.PHON: nu qaqətitan marəpaɭikuz aja.
S.FORM: nu qaqetitan marepalikuz aya.
S.PHON: nu qaqətitan marəpaɭikuz aja.

S.FORM: aitsu a mamazangiɫan nu qemayam tua sauzayan, papirava tua kama tua kina tua zua sirakeraketj.
S.PHON: aiʦu a mamazaŋiʎan nu qəmajam tua sauzajan, papirava tua kama tua kina tua zua sirakərakəc.
S.FORM: aicu a mamazangiljan nu qemayam tua sauzayan, papirava tua kama tua kina tua zua sirakeraketj.
S.PHON: aiʦu a mamazaŋiʎan nu qəmajam tua sauzajan, papirava tua kama tua kina tua zua sirakərakəʦ.
S.TRANSL: In the case of the chieftains when they inspect the possessions offered as a bride price, they dress the father and the mother of (the bride) the cause of all-day celebrations.

S.FORM: nu uqaɫay gamuts ḍeqer tjakit kuang siatj paledeled a sipapirava.
S.PHON: nu uqaʎaj gamuʦ ɖəqər cakit kuaŋ siac paɭədəɭəd a sipapirava.
S.FORM: nu uqaljay gamuc dreqer tjakit kuang siatj paledeled a sipapirava.
S.PHON: nu uqaʎaj gamuʦ ɖəqər ʦakit kuaŋ siaʦ paɭədəɭəd a sipapirava.
S.TRANSL: A male is dressed in a headdress (with wild boars’ tusks), necklace, sword, gun, bandolier and belt.

S.FORM: nu vavayan quvaɫ tarar vatsvats ḍeqer kava kun tjukap a sipapirava.
S.PHON: nu vavajan quvaʎ tarar vaʦvaʦ ɖəqər kava kun cukap a sipapirava.
S.FORM: nu vavayan quvalj tarar vacvac dreqer kava kun tjukap a sipapirava.
S.PHON: nu vavajan quvaʎ tarar vaʦvaʦ ɖəqər kava kun ʦukap a sipapirava.
S.TRANSL: A female is dressed in a fur coat, turban, pendant earrings, necklace, robe, skirt and shoes.

S.FORM: qau nu kavusaman papiravan a kama a kina azua aɫak a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau nu kavusaman papiravan a kama a kina azua aʎak a vavajan.
S.FORM: qau nu kavusaman papiravan a kama a kina azua aljak a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau nu kavusaman papiravan a kama a kina azua aʎak a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: And when it is a head chieftain father, mother and daughter are all dressed.

S.FORM: ɫakua inika mapasusu a penuɫat.
S.PHON: ʎakua inika mapasusu a pənuʎat.
S.FORM: ljakua inika mapasusu a penuljat.
S.PHON: ʎakua inika mapasusu a pənuʎat.
S.TRANSL: But these customs are not always carried out.

S.FORM: sauzayan anga a mamadjeledjelean, paisu anga a sipapirava.
S.PHON: sauzajan aŋa a mamaɉəɭəɉəɭəan, paisu aŋa a sipapirava.
S.FORM: sauzayan anga a mamadjeledjelean, paisu anga a sipapirava.
S.PHON: sauzajan aŋa a mamaɉəɭəɉəɭəan, paisu aŋa a sipapirava.
S.TRANSL: Cheaper possessions or money may be given instead of dressing the people.

S.FORM: nu uri marepaukuz seman rarukuzan nu katiatiaw, sana parepaukuz.
S.PHON: nu uri marəpaukuz səman rarukuzan nu katiatiaw, sana parəpaukuz.
S.FORM: nu uri marepaukuz seman rarukuzan nu katiatiaw, sana parepaukuz.
S.PHON: nu uri marəpaukuz səman rarukuzan nu katiatiaw, sana parəpaukuz.
S.TRANSL: When a wedding is to take place, preparations of food etc (which confirm the agreement(?)) are made the day before, and then the wedding goes ahead.

S.FORM: nu maɫianga marepakaiv, sana ɫayuz.
S.PHON: nu maʎiaŋa marəpakaiv, sana ʎajuz.
S.FORM: nu maljianga marepakaiv, sana ljayuz.
S.PHON: nu maʎiaŋa marəpakaiv, sana ʎajuz.
S.TRANSL: Next day there is an exchange of drinks (at a feast); then there is a love song (at which the bride weeps).

S.FORM: nu ɫemayuz mana siqunevunevuɫ a sangasangasan.
S.PHON: nu ʎəmajuz mana siqunəvunəvuʎ a saŋasaŋasan.
S.FORM: nu ljemayuz mana siqunevunevulj a sangasangasan.
S.PHON: nu ʎəmajuz mana siqunəvunəvuʎ a saŋasaŋasan.
S.TRANSL: When the song is sung there is the first distribution of small gifts to all.

S.FORM: nu maɫianga kiulaw anga a sema tsemtsemel.
S.PHON: nu maʎiaŋa kiuɭaw aŋa a səma ʦəmʦəməɭ.
S.FORM: nu maljianga kiulaw anga a sema cemcemel.
S.PHON: nu maʎiaŋa kiuɭaw aŋa a səma ʦəmʦəməɭ.
S.TRANSL: The next day the bride hides herself in the woods.

S.FORM: nu masulem nu mangtjez anga qemunevuɫ anga uta tua sikinmusaɫ.
S.PHON: nu masuɭəm nu maŋcəz aŋa qəmunəvuʎ aŋa uta tua sikinmusaʎ.
S.FORM: nu masulem nu mangtjez anga qemunevulj anga uta tua sikinmusalj.
S.PHON: nu masuɭəm nu maŋʦəz aŋa qəmunəvuʎ aŋa uta tua sikinmusaʎ.
S.TRANSL: In the evening when she comes back there is the second distribution of gifts.

S.FORM: nu maɫian anga nu qadavan kitsaqis anga, mana zua viliviliɫan anga a qemunevuɫ; sana kialap nu maɫian anga nu qadavan.
S.PHON: nu maʎian aŋa nu qadavan kiʦaqis aŋa, mana zua viɭiviɭiʎan aŋa a qəmunəvuʎ; sana kiaɭap nu maʎian aŋa nu qadavan.
S.FORM: nu maljian anga nu qadavan kicaqis anga, mana zua vilivililjan anga a qemunevulj; sana kialap nu maljian anga nu qadavan.
S.PHON: nu maʎian aŋa nu qadavan kiʦaqis aŋa, mana zua viɭiviɭiʎan aŋa a qəmunəvuʎ; sana kiaɭap nu maʎian aŋa nu qadavan.
S.TRANSL: On the next day the bride puts on layers of underpants, and there is a last distribution of gifts. And then on the next day the bride is taken to the bridegroom’s house.

S.FORM: namayatutsu a kakudan nu tja suqatsan.
S.PHON: namajatuʦu a kakudan nu ca suqaʦan.
S.FORM: namayatucu a kakudan nu tja suqacan.
S.PHON: namajatuʦu a kakudan nu ʦa suqaʦan.
S.TRANSL: These are the customs in brief.

S.FORM: ɫakua nu i tjuqaɫa a vavayan, izua uta a kiniqayaman, a pana a tjekeza, a siniteperuk tua qinaɫan, a tedek tua tsakar, na maqatsuvutsuvung, a sitjumaq, a sinipakizing.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu i cuqaʎa a vavajan, izua uta a kiniqajaman, a pana a cəkəza, a sinitəpəruk tua qinaʎan, a tədək tua ʦakar, na maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ, a sicumaq, a sinipakiziŋ.
S.FORM: ljakua nu i tjuqalja a vavayan, izua uta a kiniqayaman, a pana a tjekeza, a siniteperuk tua qinaljan, a tedek tua cakar, na maqacuvucuvung, a sitjumaq, a sinipakizing.
S.PHON: ʎakua nu i ʦuqaʎa a vavajan, izua uta a kiniqajaman, a pana a ʦəkəza, a sinitəpəruk tua qinaʎan, a tədək tua ʦakar, na maqaʦuvuʦuvuŋ, a siʦumaq, a sinipakiziŋ.
S.TRANSL: But if the bride is in another village there will also be inspections of rivers and bridges, and compensation for ‘breaking up the village’, decorations for the shrine, the youths’ share, compensation for entering(?) and for food expenses (or for bringing home)(?).

S.FORM: aitsu a siputseketsekeɫ nua mamazangiɫan kadjunangan pana qaɫuqaɫupan djilung a kauɫayan qata kuang tjakit, izuanan uta a zuma vadis.
S.PHON: aiʦu a sipuʦəkəʦəkəʎ nua mamazaŋiʎan kaɉunaŋan pana qaʎuqaʎupan ɉiɭuŋ a kauʎajan qata kuaŋ cakit, izuanan uta a zuma vadis.
S.FORM: aicu a sipucekecekelj nua mamazangiljan kadjunangan pana qaljuqaljupan djilung a kauljayan qata kuang tjakit, izuanan uta a zuma vadis.
S.PHON: aiʦu a sipuʦəkəʦəkəʎ nua mamazaŋiʎan kaɉunaŋan pana qaʎuqaʎupan ɉiɭuŋ a kauʎajan qata kuaŋ ʦakit, izuanan uta a zuma vadis.
S.TRANSL: The bride-prices among chieftains include land, rivers, hunting grounds, precious pots, beads, guns and swords; and there is other tribute to be paid as well.

S.FORM: izua uta aza tjiuma ayayain a ɫaɫakayan.
S.PHON: izua uta aza ciuma ajajain a ʎaʎakajan.
S.FORM: izua uta aza tjiuma ayayain a ljaljakayan.
S.PHON: izua uta aza ʦiuma ajajain a ʎaʎakajan.
S.TRANSL: There is also a swing set up called a tjiuma.
